select_when: Outcomes should shape the next action and the system can observe consequences quickly enough to learn instead of repeating the same move blindly.
Behavior changes the environment and the environment changes behavior back, so the problem depends on circular rather than one-way causality.
Measurement can actually close the loop because signals are tied to real levers and the next adjustment can follow from what the system just produced.
The work happens inside a complex dynamic system where delays, adaptation, and iteration matter more than a static snapshot.
avoid_when: The organization is collecting signal but never translating it into changed behavior, priorities, or design.
The loop is being treated as progress even though the team is stuck in endless questioning or one-way communication that never closes into action.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on closed-loop adaptation, signal use, and circular causality rather than generalized iteration rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor agent-architecture or product-analytics overlays where the raw source already gives stronger system-loop logic.
Wave 4 candidacy stays observational only for this batch.
open_questions: The output label compresses several raw roles: iterative refinement, diagnostic revision, and action-closing implementation discipline.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on adaptive signals, circular causality, and loop closure; review mostly compresses those themes into routing triggers.

avoid_when.note: The first condition is explicit in the raw danger line and the second conservatively compresses the raw warnings on endless loops and top-down stuck recordings.

input_type.note: The source clearly operates on systems receiving signals and adapting, but the typed input label remains reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw model closes loops through iteration, diagnosis, and changed action; the output label compresses that into an operational intervention type.

reasoning_types.note: Feedback loops are clearly causal and systems-oriented in use, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
